TREES DOWN AROUND APPROXIMATELY SIX HOMES ON RAMSGATE ROAD, IN THE COFFEE BLUFF AREA. ONE HOME SUSTAINED SOME MINOR DAMAGE FROM AN OAK TREE PARTIALLY FALLING ON IT.
TREES DOWN ON RAMSGATE ROAD, IN THE COFFEE BLUFF AREA.
SCREVEN COUNTY 911 CENTER REPORTED 2 TREES DOWN AT PINE GROVE INN ROAD AND BURTON'S FERRY ROAD. THIS WAS DUE TO A POSSIBLE TORNADO.

*** 1 INJ *** NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED THAT A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF BLUFF AND BEST LOOP ROADS. THE TORNADO TRACKED NORTHEASTWARD, PRODUCING EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE. THE TORNADO ALSO COMPLETELY DESTROYED SOME MOBILE HOMES ALONG FITTS AND ST. MARKS ROADS, WHERE ONE PERSON WAS INJURED. THIS DAMAGE WAS THE BASIS FOR THE EF-3 RATING. THE TORNADO THEN CONTINUED TO DAMAGE MANY TREES AND SOME STRUCTURES SOUTH AND EAST OF ALLENDALE.
TREES DOWN ON FITTS RD NEAR INTERSECTION WITH BLUFF RD.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED A DESTROYED MOBILE HOME.
HOUSE DESTROYED AND LOTS OF TREES DOWN AT ST MARK'S RD AND BLUFF RD.
ALLENDALE COUNTY 911 CENTER RELAYED A FIRE FIGHTER REPORT OF A TORNADO NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF U.S. HIGHWAY 301 AND REVOLUTIONARY TRAIL. NO DAMAGE REPORT WAS AVAILABLE.
ALLENDALE COUNTY 991 CENTER REPORTED ROOFS BLOWN OFF STRUCTURES IN ALLENDALE. TIME WAS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR DATA.
*** 1 INJ *** ALLENDALE COUNTY 911 CENTER REPORTED AN INJURY IN ALLENDALE DURING THE TUESDAY AFTERNOON TORNADO.
SEVERAL COMMERCIAL BUILDINGS SEVERELY DAMAGED ALONG HWY 278 SOUTHEAST OF ALLENDALE. SEVERAL LARGE TREES DOWNED OR SPLIT. A LARGE TANK WAS BLOWN OVER.

NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED THAT AN EF-3 TORNADO WITH A PATH LENGTH OF JUST OVER 11 MILES AND A WIDTH OF UP TO 1300 YARDS CONTINUED TO PRODUCE EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE EAST OF ALLENDALE BEFORE IT DISSIPATED NEAR WILLIAMS ROAD NORTHEAST OF ULMER.
THE EVANS COUNTY 911 CENTER REPORTED A TREE DOWN ON SOUTH GRADY STREET. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
FIRE DEPT REPORTED TORNADO ON THE GROUND WITH DAMAGE. THE COURTHOUSE AND THE COUNTY JAIL WERE DAMAGED.

*** 1 FATAL, 12 INJ *** NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED THAT A TORNADO OF AT LEAST EF-3 INTENSITY TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE BRYAN COUNTY COURTHOUSE IN PEMBROKE AND TRACKED ACROSS NORTHERN BRYAN COUNTY ALONG A PATH OVER 11 MILES LONG. THE TORNADO REACHED MAXIMUM INTENSITY IN THE PARK PLACE SUBDIVISION AREA, WHERE SEVERAL STRUCTURES WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED AND WHERE THE TORNADO WIDTH REACHED THREE-QUARTERS OF A MILE. THE TORNADO ALSO COMPLETELY DESTROYED SEVERAL MOBILE HOMES ALONG HOMESTEAD ROAD WHERE 1 FATALITY OCCURRED.
*** 1 FATAL, 12 INJ *** BROADCAST MEDIA RELAYED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA A REPORT THAT 1 PERSON WAS KILLED DURING A TORNADO IN PEMBROKE.
*** 1 FATAL *** BROADCAST MEDIA RELAYED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA A REPORT THAT 1 PERSON WAS KILLED DURING A TORNADO IN PEMBROKE.
FIRE DEPT REPORTED TORNADO ON THE GROUND WITH DAMAGE.

DAMAGE REPORTED TO VEHICLES.
NUMEROUS HOMES DAMAGED AND MULTIPLE OCCUPANTS TRAPPED IN PARK PLACE SUBDIVISION NEAR ELLABELL.

*** 1 FATAL, 12 INJ *** EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO THE PARK PLACE SUBDIVISION WITH NUMEROUS INJURIES. ONE FATALITY IN THE HOMESTEAD PARK NEIGHBORHOOD WHERE NUMEROUS HOMES WERE DAMAGED. WILMA EDWARDS RD WAS COMPLETELY IMPASSABLE DUE TO DOWNED TREES AND POWER LINES. RECREATION CENTER AT HENDRIX PARK DAMAGED.

NWS SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR STATE ROUTE 300 JUST NORTH OF ULMER. THE TORNADO TRACKED RAPIDLY INTO BAMBURG COUNTY WHERE IT ATTAINED EF-3 INTENSITY.
SOCIAL MEDIA VIDEO SHOWS A TORNADO CROSSING HWY 300 NEAR INTERSECTION WITH HWY 301 IN ULMER. TREE AND STRUCTURAL DEBRIS CAN BE SEEN BLOWING ACROSS THE ROAD.
NWS RADAR INDICATED A TORNADO DEBRIS SIGNATURE IN EXTREME NORTHEASTERN ALLENDALE COUNTY.

NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF PEPPER STREET IN VANRVILLE SC. THE TORNADO TRAVELLED NORTHEAST ALONG A PATH 1.75 MILES LONG AND 250 YARDS WIDE, CAUSING MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOME, SNAPPING AND UPROOTING MULTIPLE TREES, OVERTURNING SOME UTILITY TRAILERS AND THROWING A SMALL GRAIN BIN. THE TORNADO ENDED NEAR PAPAS ROAD AT 624 PM EDT.

HAMPTON COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED A TORNADO TOUCH DOWN ALONG PAPAS ROAD IN EASTERN HAMPTON COUNTY. A GRAIN SILO WAS LOFTED AND CANNOT BE LOCATED. MULTIPLE LARGE TREES SNAPPED. A DOG KENNEL WAS DESTROYED. OTHER OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED.

NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF ALISON LANE. THE TORNADO TRAVELLED EAST ALONG A PATH JUST OVER 1 MILE LONG AND 100 YARDS WIDE. THE TORNADO SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES ALONG THIS PATH AND DESTROYED A FENCE BEFORE ENDING JUST EAST OF SANDY SPRINGS CIRCLE.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY DETERMINED THAT 70-75 MPH STRAIGHT-LINE THUNDERSTORM WINDS IMPACTED PARTS OF THE WANDO WOODS SUBDIVISION. MOST CONCENTRATED DAMAGE WAS NOTED ALONG FLYNN DR WEST OF HOLBIRD ST, WHERE NUMEROUS MEDIUM TO LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE SNAPPED OFF. FLYING DEBRIS BROKE A FEW WINDOWS AND PRODUCED MINOR SHINGLE AND EXTERIOR DAMAGE TO NORTH AND WEST FACING SURFACES OF A FEW HOMES. ALSO, A LARGE TRAMPOLINE WAS PROPELLED 150 FEET TOWARD THE SOUTHEAST AND DESTROYED.

PUBLIC PHONED IN AN ADDITIONAL REPORT FROM FLYNN ROAD IN WANDO WOODS SUBDIVISION NEAR THE ASHLEY RIVER. SEVERAL TREES AND NUMEROUS LARGE TREE BRANCHES WERE BLOWN DOWN. SEVERAL RESIDENCES SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE DUE TO STRONG WINDS AND FLYING DEBRIS. DAMAGE INCLUDED BROKEN WINDOWS, AS WELL AS MISSING ROOF SHINGLES AND PIECES OF SIDING.
